channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: US Embassy in Kyiv shuts down after receiving warning of 'significant' Russian air attack
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The U.S. Embassy in Kyiv said it has received warning of a potentially significant Russian air attack on Wednesday and would be closed as a precaution. In a statement, the embassy also instructed employees to shelter in place and also recommended that U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump has called for dismantling the Education Department. Here's what that would mean
item.description: Throughout his campaign, President-elect Donald Trump heaped scorn on the federal Department of Education, describing it as being infiltrated by “radicals, zealots and Marxists.”.
item.dc:creator: Annie Ma, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Diddy hit with 5 new lawsuits, including alleged teen sexual assault
item.description: A woman says that at 17 years old, she attended a Fourth of July party in 2004 where Combs sexually assaulted her while she was unconscious.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Texas board advances plan to allow Bible material in elementary school lessons
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas' education board on Tuesday advanced a new Bible-infused curriculum that would be optional for schools to incorporate in kindergarten through fifth grades, one of the latest Republican-led efforts in the U.S. to incorporate more religious teaching into classrooms.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reuters hires former Post and AP executive editor Sally Buzbee to lead US, Canada news operation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sally Buzbee, the former executive editor of The Washington Post and The Associated Press, is joining Reuters as the news agency's top editor for the United States and Canada, the company said Tuesday. As news editor for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minimum wage isn't going up: California votes against Prop 32
item.description: Californians are projected to vote against a minimum wage boost in the state, giving a "no" to Proposition 32, according to an Associated Press race call on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Dom McAndrew, Fox 5

item.title: The dark energy pushing our universe apart may not be what it seems, scientists say
item.description: Distant, ancient galaxies are giving scientists more hints that a mysterious force called dark energy may not be what they thought.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trial starts for New Hampshire youth center worker accused of holding down boy while he was raped
item.description: MANCHESTER, N.H. (AP) — A man who says he was brutally raped as a teenager by workers at a New Hampshire youth center testified Tuesday that he couldn't report the attack at the time because the perpetrators were responsible for his welfare. “They just did that to you.
item.dc:creator: NICK PERRY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: When Trump left office, students were struggling due of the pandemic. They still are
item.description: Learning loss and chronic absenteeism are proving to be stubborn problems, and education experts say Trump is currently more focused on culture war issues.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: 7-Eleven shoplifter attacks employee with improvised flamethrower
item.description: That employee confronted the shoplifter, at which point she produced a lighter and an aerosol can, lighting the spray to create a makeshift flamethrower.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Today's entry-level employees not ready for the job, survey of executives says
item.description: (KRON) -- Entry-level Gen Z workers aren't prepared for the demands of the modern workplace, according to a majority of executives and workers surveyed in a new report.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Jeffries: Republicans don't have a mandate
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Tuesday rejected the idea that voters delivered an Election Day mandate to Donald Trump and the Republicans, arguing that the GOP’s razor-thin majority in the House means that nothing will get done in Washington without Democratic support.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: National Geographic names 2024 Pictures of the Year
item.description: Emperor penguins taking the plunge, an Arkansas family experiencing a total solar eclipse, and a scientist holding a rare white rhino fetus are among the most striking and important images captured this year, according to National Geographic.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Carcass of endangered fin whale washes up near Alaska's largest city
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — An endangered fin whale that washed up near a coastal trail in Alaska's largest city has attracted curious onlookers while biologists seek answers as to what caused the animal's death.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hacker gains access to testimony in Gaetz investigations: reports
item.description: A hacker gained access to witness testimony related to investigations into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), who resigned from Congress last week after President-elect Trump tapped him for attorney general, multiple news outlets reported Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, WFLA, NBC

item.title: John Stamos wears bald cap to show support for Dave Coulier amid cancer diagnosis
item.description: John Stamos is showing his support for his former "Full House" co-star, Dave Coulier, amid his cancer diagnosis.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: Mississippi restaurant, co-owner sentenced for decades-long fish mislabeling scheme
item.description: GULFPORT, Miss. (WJTV) - A Mississippi corporation operating as Mary Mahoney’s Old French House restaurant in Biloxi, Mississippi, and its co-owner and manager were sentenced on charges for a long-standing conspiracy to misbrand seafood.
item.dc:creator: Sethanie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: The White House's Christmas tree is a symbol of resilience for hurricane-hit North Carolina farms
item.description: NEWLAND, N.C. (AP) — The Cartner family had known since last year that one of their farm’s Christmas trees would be headed to the White House this winter.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Reuters hires former Post and AP executive editor Sally Buzbee to lead US, Canada news operation
item.description: Reuters has hired Sally Buzbee, former executive editor of The Washington Post and The Associated Press, to be its top news editor in the United States and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump watches as Musk’s SpaceX launches Starship rocket
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is expected to attend the launch of a SpaceX rocket in Texas on Tuesday, sources familiar with the plans confirmed, in the latest sign of Trump’s closeness to the company's billionaire CEO Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Speaker Johnson becomes 'MAGA' Mike, cementing place in Trump's circle
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is cementing his place in President-elect Trump’s inner orbit, becoming not only a visible — if slightly out-of-place-looking — part of his entourage, but a key supporter of his no-holds-barred approach to his second term.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9

item.title: Utah authorities offering $30k reward for information on shooting death of wild horse
item.description: A federally protected wild horse, identified as Pyrite/Goldie/Glory by the Onaqui Catalogue, was found shot to death in the Onaqui Heard Management Area by a member of the public.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Laken Riley murder: Family of slain Georgia student sobs in court as witnesses describe crime scene evidence
item.description: Georgia prosecutors placed Jose Ibarra, the suspect accused of murdering Augusta University nursing student Laken Riley, at the scene of the crime on the morning of Feb. 22.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Riders caught on camera stuck in midair on California amusement park ride
item.description: Knott's Berry Farm officials said that several riders got stuck on one of theme park’s rides for over two hours Monday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bear shot, killed by homeowner in Southern California
item.description: A California man is being criticized after he shot and killed a bear in his yard last week that he claimed was getting into his chicken coop.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Diddy tried to reach out to witnesses from jail, prosecutors allege
item.description: Combs has repeatedly "flouted rules" so he can "improperly impact the outcome of his case," prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Kelley Connors, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The 9 GOP senators who could derail Trump’s Cabinet picks
item.description: Trump can afford three defections within the Senate GOP conference and still get his picks confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Triathlete with terminal cancer completes Ironman championship in Hawaii
item.description: An Ironman is a test of endurance featuring a 2.4-mile swim, a 26.2-mile marathon run, and a 112-mile bike ride.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Spirit Airlines files for bankruptcy as financial losses pile up
item.description: Spirit Airlines said Monday that it has filed for bankruptcy protection and will attempt to reboot as it struggles to recover from the pandemic-caused swoon in travel and a failed attempt to sell the airline to JetBlue. Spirit, the biggest U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KOENIG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Union workers picket for 3rd day at Las Vegas casino with no talks slated
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Picketing continued Sunday outside a hotel-casino near the Las Vegas Strip that remained open with no talks scheduled between management and union members striking for a new contract.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US-supplied long-range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: President Joe Biden has authorized the use of U.S.-supplied long-range missiles by Ukraine to strike even deeper inside Russia, the latest easing of limitations meant to prevent the conflict from further spiraling, according to one U.S. official and three people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Longest-waiting child in Arkansas foster care system finds forever family
item.description: After more than 6,100 days in the Arkansas foster care system, 17-year-old Jose has now found his forever family.
item.dc:creator: Mattison Gafner, WGN 9

item.title: Trump attends UFC championship fight in New York, taking break from Cabinet picks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump walked out to a roaring standing ovation just ahead of the start of the UFC pay-per-view card at Madison Square Garden on Saturday night, combining two things close to his heart: fierce battles inside the octagon and New York City.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, WFLA, NBC
